report_name: Email Under Siege: AI, QR Codes, and Phishing-as-a-Service Fuel Surge in Attacks

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use PDF and HTML attachments to deliver malicious content. A novel technique involves embedding QR codes within PDF attachments to bypass traditional email security filters and lead users to phishing sites on their mobile devices.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The ultimate goal of the QR code phishing attacks is to direct the user to a malicious credential harvesting link on their mobile device, bypassing corporate desktop security.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The attack's success relies on the user opening the PDF attachment and then choosing to scan the embedded QR code, which directs them to a phishing site.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Following successful credential phishing, attackers use the compromised legitimate accounts to send highly convincing internal phishing emails, leveraging the implicit trust associated with the sender's identity.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Social Engineering LLM
attack_patterns.description: Attackers leverage Artificial Intelligence to craft highly convincing and personalized social engineering lures at scale, increasing the effectiveness of their phishing campaigns.

tools.name: Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS)
tools.description: PhaaS platforms and kits are used to industrialize and scale phishing campaigns, providing the infrastructure, templates, and management for high-volume attacks. 90% of high-volume campaigns utilized PhaaS kits.

tools.name: Artificial Intelligence
tools.description: AI is used by attackers as a force multiplier to craft hyper-realistic, personalized social engineering lures at scale for use in sophisticated phishing emails.

identities.name: General Corporate Environments
identities.description: General companies targeted by large-scale, industrialized phishing campaigns. The report states 34% of companies experience at least one account compromise each month.

infrastructure.name: Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure provided by PhaaS platforms for hosting phishing campaigns, including templates and management interfaces.

infrastructure.name: QR Code Phishing Landing Page
infrastructure.description: Credential harvesting websites delivered via QR codes embedded in PDF attachments, designed to capture user credentials on mobile devices.

indicators.name: Suspicious PDF Attachment with QR Code Keywords
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PDF Attachment with QR Code Keywords
id: 2b8e1c9a-4f5d-4b6e-8c7a-9d0f1e2d3c4b
status: experimental
description: Detects emails containing PDF attachments and keywords related to QR codes, which could indicate a QR code phishing attempt as described in the 2026 Barracuda report.
references:
  - https://www.barracuda.com/news-release/ai-and-phishing-as-a-service-drive-increase-in-email-attacks-barracuda-reports
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate business communications involving QR codes in PDFs (e.g., event tickets, marketing materials).
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection_keywords:
    Body|contains:
      - 'QR code'
      - 'scan to view'
      - 'scan to verify'
  selection_attachment:
    AttachmentName|endswith: '.pdf'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects emails that contain a PDF attachment and also include keywords in the body commonly used to socially engineer a user into scanning a QR code. This pattern matches the QR code phishing tactic highlighted in the report.

indicators.name: Suspicious HTML Email Attachment
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious HTML Email Attachment
id: 8a9b0c1d-2e3f-4a5b-6c7d-8e9f0a1b2c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects emails with HTML attachments, which can be used to deliver obfuscated phishing links or scripts, a tactic highlighted in the 2026 Barracuda report.
references:
  - https://www.barracuda.com/news-release/ai-and-phishing-as-a-service-drive-increase-in-email-attacks-barracuda-reports
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate business communications that use HTML attachments. This rule may need to be tuned to exclude known-good senders or internal traffic.
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    AttachmentName|endswith:
      - '.html'
      - '.htm'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects emails containing HTML attachments, which are noted in the report as an evasive delivery tactic for phishing and malicious scripts.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious PDF Attachment with QR Code Keywords
